name: Academia MCP Server (arXiv + ACL)
what_it_does: MCP server by IlyaGusev (notable NLP researcher) for academic literature search. Enables AI agents to search arXiv preprints and ACL Anthology (NLP/linguistics papers), retrieve citations and references, browse related web sources, and accelerate research workflows. Purpose-built for ML/NLP research acceleration.
use_cases: Searching arXiv for recent ML/AI papers from research assistant agents
Finding NLP papers via ACL Anthology from linguistics and language model research agents
Retrieving citation graphs and paper references from literature review agents
Accelerating academic research workflows with AI-powered paper discovery
not_for: Searching non-academic web content (use general search MCPs)
Accessing paywalled journal articles (arXiv and ACL are open access)
Real-time news or recent events (academic preprint focus)
best_when: An ML/AI/NLP researcher or research agent needs to search academic literature — primarily arXiv preprints and ACL papers — with citation graph navigation and reference retrieval.
avoid_when: You need broad academic search beyond arXiv/ACL (use Semantic Scholar or Google Scholar APIs for wider coverage). Also: ACL Anthology is NLP/linguistics-specific.

auth.notes: No authentication required. arXiv and ACL Anthology are free public APIs. No API key needed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — arXiv and ACL are open access with public APIs
pricing.notes: Completely free. arXiv and ACL Anthology are open academic resources. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: arXiv API: 3 requests/second; ACL has informal limits — be respectful

known_agent_gotchas: arXiv rate limit is 3 requests/second — bulk paper retrieval needs throttling
arXiv search returns preprints — may not be peer-reviewed; cite appropriately
ACL Anthology coverage is NLP/computational linguistics only — not general CS or medicine
IlyaGusev is a credible NLP researcher (Saiga, Rulm models) — quality implementation for research use
agent_readiness.security_notes: Public academic APIs — no sensitive data. HTTPS to arXiv and ACL. No credentials required. Safe for research agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public APIs with no auth. No credentials to protect. Safe read-only academic data access.
